item.title: NRL heavyweight Melbourne Storm in 'positive’ talks with Tasmanian government to bring games south
item.description: Melbourne Storm chief executive Justin Rodski says he's "got no doubt" a deal with Tasmania can be reached if the commercial interests line up, following a round of courting from premier Jeremy Rockliff.

item.title: Israel's military crossed a tank over a major Lebanese river. We found where
item.description: Images of the first tanks to roll over Lebanon's Litani River were posted online this week. ABC NEWS Verify has found the spot where Israel's military crossed.

item.title: Ash Gardner shines for Australia in ICC T20 Women's World Cup warm up
item.description: Ash Gardner will hit Australia's Twenty20 World Cup warm-up matches full of confidence after a half-century in the last of three informal hit-outs against South Africa.

item.title: TGA still investigating sunscreens that Choice testing found failed to meet SPF claims
item.description: Most of the sunscreens that failed to meet their SPF label claims, according to independent testing, are still under investigation by the regulator one year later.

item.title: In pictures: Princess Catherine shares emotional moment with cancer patient
item.description: The Princess of Wales joined in a hospital cancer ward celebration for a young mum who completed chemotherapy.

item.title: Princess of Wales hugs cancer patient who has finished treatment
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What to know about the New World screwworm fly and its reappearance in the US
item.description: The New World screwworm fly is threatening the $US113 billion United States cattle industry for the first time in more than a half century.

item.title: Marjane Satrapi, Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker known for Persepolis, 'dies of sadness' at the age of 56
item.description: A committed advocate to furthering free speech and women's rights in her birth country, Satrapi lost her husband, Swedish actor Mattias Ripa, in April last year, and "died of sadness", according to French media.

item.title: Kitten left for dead after being thrown from car finds new home with rescuers
item.description: A kitten left for dead after being thrown from a moving car has spent months recovering and is now at a new home with two familiar faces.

item.title: Matildas star Mary Fowler wants to make an impact and have fun along the way
item.description: Matildas star Mary Fowler is using her platform to share what matters to her, as she navigates becoming a truly global footballing superstar.

item.title: Energy prices to fall across Queensland after national drop in prices
item.description: Household power bills will drop by about 7 per cent, while business bills will drop by 8 per cent from July 1.

item.title: NSW Police officer Benedict Bryant sentenced over death of Indigenous teenager Jai Wright
item.description: A NSW Police officer convicted over a fatal collision that killed an Indigenous teenager has avoided time behind bars.

item.title: New Zealand soccer player Tim Payne meets social media influencer who made him a celebrity
item.description: New Zealand soccer player Tim Payne has met social media influencer Valen Scarsini after the Argentine encouraged his fans to follow the unheralded All Whites player.

item.title: 100,000 exotic cockroaches allegedly seized from commercial breeder
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hidden cosmic web revealed by WA radio telescope with biggest-ever magnetic map
item.description: Data gathered by scientists at a radio observatory in WA's Murchison has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

item.title: Future of iconic wine label Wolf Blass unclear amid Treasury Wine Estates restructure
item.description: A cloud hangs over the future of the famous Barossa brand as its maker, Treasury Wine Estates, looks to cull more than half of its labels.

item.title: Exploring the link between gardening and better mental health
item.description: While gardening alone is not a treatment for mental illness, a growing body of evidence links it with higher levels of mental wellbeing and life satisfaction.

item.title: Please explain: One Nation housing policy confuses its own as senator's interview turns into a 'train wreck'
item.description: After Barnaby Joyce needed a do-over interview to explain One Nation's housing policy, a senator's interview was terminated after it turned into a 'train wreck'.

item.title: Mona visitor numbers are up, but gambling continues to prop up loss-making Tasmanian gallery
item.description: Newly filed accounts give a first glimpse of Mona's ultimate holding company, showing the loss-making gallery remains heavily reliant on gambling funds.

item.title: Albanese admits One Nation's rise partly why he moved to change negative gearing and capital gains tax
item.description: The rise of One Nation is partly behind the government's decision to take on tax reform in this year's budget, the prime minister has admitted.

item.title: Ponga injured in Knights' loss ahead of Origin II
item.description: Maroons fullback Kalyn Ponga was left "very stiff and sore" after sustaining an injury in the Knights' 32-30 loss to the Storm in Melbourne.

item.title: Bulldogs defender calls out abusive fan after fiery clash with Hawks star
item.description: Michael Sellwood has the last laugh against opponent Nick Watson and a spectator, as the Western Bulldogs toppled Hawthorn in another tense finish.

item.title: ACT Brumbies knocked out of Super Rugby Pacific finals by dominant Hurricanes
item.description: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week in first visit since 2019
item.description: Kim Jong Un has been trying to improve ties with China, North Korea's biggest trading partner and provider of aid.

item.title: Putin rejects offer to meet Zelenskyy as European leaders warn Ukraine war poses 'direct threat' to nearby nations
item.description: A Ukrainian naval drone has exploded in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta, as European leaders warned the war in Ukraine is becoming a "direct threat" to countries along the EU's eastern border.

item.title: Matt Corby on new album Tragic Magic, returning to live TV and writing Brother
item.description: He got his start as a singing TV idol, but Matt Corby is making music for himself now, finding the magic in grief, growth and family.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of a 60s rock classic, a famous poem and Euphoria vs Hacks
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Zoe Pepper and Fiona Wright take on the housing crisis in Birthright and Kill Your Boomers
item.description: Zoe Pepper and Fiona Wright are among the millennial artists using their work to tackle the housing crisis, which has seen house prices rise by 400 per cent in 25 years.

item.title: Medical technology sector raises fresh concerns with Labor’s capital gains tax overhaul
item.description: Medical technology companies say new restrictions on tax refunds for research and development will hurt health startups in Australia.

item.title: The last holding place
item.description: When Tammy Shipley felt herself sliding into psychosis, she made a heartbreaking decision: She walked into a police station and asked to be arrested, believing prison would keep her safe. Here's what happened next.

item.title: 'Volcano on the sea floor': Labor is aware of the challenges posed by One Nation, budget
item.description: This week the Coalition's weakness in Question Time reminded onlookers of Albanese's command of the Westminster system, while Labor insiders expressed their anxieties about One Nation's polling surge.

item.title: Mates team up with NSW Riverina farmers to help feed people in Sudan
item.description: An unlikely pair from the NSW Riverina plan to ship 100 tonnes of wheat to war-torn Sudan.

item.title: Fake police scam targets Chinese diaspora with threats and forged documents
item.description: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

item.title: Alexander Zverev beats Jakub Menšík to reach French Open final and Flavio Cobolli advances
item.description: Alexander Zverev moves to within touching distance of a long-coveted major title, while Flavio Cobolli reaches the final after Matteo Arnaldi withdraws due to illness.

item.title: Isolated showers expected to return to parts of Queensland this weekend
item.description: Settled, clear and cool conditions will continue into Queensland's first weekend of winter, with showers brewing from late Sunday.

item.title: Underbool community hooked on coffee van that opens for 20 minutes a day
item.description: Residents of the tiny town of Underbool, in Victoria's remote Mallee region, flock to this small coffee van, which has even smaller opening hours.

item.title: A 'mansion tax' would likely see affluent Bulimba homeowners pay up, but some of them support it
item.description: Amid some of the most drastic proposed changes to Australia's tax system in decades and years of skyrocketing home values, one leading expert says it is an idea "worth considering".

item.title: How South Korea's plan for nuclear-powered submarines compares to AUKUS
item.description: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

item.title: Ecologist raises environmental concerns after boat runs aground in northern NSW
item.description: Excavators have been used to salvage a vessel that ran aground at Flat Rock just north of Ballina, potentially disrupting the intertidal ecosystem in the area.

item.title: Treasurer worried about hospital cost blowout amid projected record debt
item.description: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

item.title: NSW Premier Chris Minns thinks One Nation is 'genuine threat' to Labor and to his seat
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns is enjoying growing support in his electorate, but is still concerned about Pauline Hanson's presence in next year's state election.

item.title: Whitsunday Skyway project at 'advanced stage' of decade-long approvals
item.description: Approvals for the 1.8-kilometre Skyway in Airlie Beach are set to be fast-tracked, but locals say consultation with the developer has been limited.

item.title: What Pompeii taught me about the sisterhood and the power of patience
item.description: A newly uncovered fresco in Pompeii tells the story of women, wine and Roman hedonism. Buried beneath the spectacle lies a lesson about restraint and why some discoveries should be left to the future.

item.title: White House to accelerate development of AI for 'war fighting' and national security
item.description: As a major AI company warns of an increased risk of "humans losing control over AI systems", the Trump administration says it will accelerate the development and use of artificial intelligence for national security applications.

item.title: How a viral post helped Ben and Naomi find love on the Finke Desert Race track
item.description: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.

item.title: Sydney's falling property prices sign of more to come, real estate agents say
item.description: Sydney property prices have dipped 0.9 per cent in the past month, following a tepid six months. But how worried should home owners be?

item.title: Government's proposed crackdown on gambling advertising doesn't go far enough for most Australians
item.description: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

item.title: First Nations rom-com brings Arnhem Land love story to Sydney Film Festival, with nod to Romeo and Juliet
item.description: In a First Nations romantic comedy debuting in Sydney this week, a new writer-director has used her lived experience to tell the story of young love in a remote Northern Territory community.

item.title: Oscar Piastri struggles in Monaco F1 Grand Prix practice as Ferrari makes statement
item.description: Oscar Piastri has failed to make an impression in the opening practice sessions at the Monaco Grand Prix.

item.title: Gas industry boom brings uneven growth to Western Downs towns
item.description: In Queensland's "energy capital", two towns on either side of the same gas fields are feeling the effects very differently.

item.title: Mother creates culturally appropriate activewear to empower women
item.description: Yasmin Jammoul was sick of wearing oversized men's T-shirts to the gym when she started exercising postpartum, so she created her own clothing line.

item.title: Farmers question decision to put Kununurra night space for youth near Ord cotton properties
item.description: The decision to locate Kununurra's new youth centre on an abandoned farm 7km from town has sparked local concerns.

item.title: US and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military says it has shot down four drones Iran has launched toward the Strait of Hormuz and struck some of Tehran's coastal surveillance radar sites in response.

item.title: Wall Street suffers worst losses in months amid massive tech stock sell-off, inflation and rate hike fears
item.description: The US stock market has experienced its worst losses in months due to large sell-offs of shares in technology giants and fears of a looming rate hike in the wake of a strong jobs figures report.

item.title: Julia de los Angeles and Angie Mead King inspire women and girls to take up motorsport in the Philippines
item.description: Julia de los Angeles loves being at the top of the Philippine motorsport scene. She sheds light on what it's like to be one of the few women among mostly men.

item.title: A dreamy, creamy chicken soup you'll want to eat out of the pot - ABC News
item.description: Looking for an easy warming meal? This creamy chicken soup has everything you need for a weeknight dinner.

item.title: Why Victoria's upper house voting regime leaves much to be desired, allowing secret backroom deals and preference swaps
item.description: Victoria's democratic system is a bit dodgy. That's not a view from a keyboard warrior, but one shared by many across the political spectrum.

item.title: Foster carers voice support for NT government's plan to change child safety laws, others oppose CLP proposal
item.description: Some foster carers have voiced support for the NT's proposed changes to child protection laws, but others say the current legislation already outlines individual safety as the paramount consideration.

item.title: How Farnsy became a figurehead for Australian protesters
item.description: Demonstrators are increasingly employing inflatable costumes and whimsy. But behind the dress up, these protesters argue it is "serious play".

item.title: AFL and Hawks stand behind Mabior Chol after racial abuse on social media
item.description: The AFL and Hawthorn come out in support of Mabior Chol after the Hawks forward shares a series of abusive messages he received after Friday night's match against the Bulldogs.

item.title: Missing Dawa Sherpa rescued after being found alive on Everest
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sherpa tells of miracle Mount Everest survival with no oxygen and just a few chocolates
item.description: A few chocolates found in his pocket and an avalanche may have been what saved Dawa Sherpa from almost certain death on Mount Everest.

item.title: More Australians targeted by Booking.com scams, with travellers lucrative targets for criminals
item.description: Australian travellers are being left out of pocket and fearful their personal data has been stolen, amid growing concerns over the security of online travel bookings.

item.title: Midfielder Connor Metcalfe 'sick of' Socceroos commentary ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Connor Metcalfe takes aim at comments coming from US experts ahead of the Socceroos' upcoming FIFA World Cup campaign.

item.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win
item.description: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

item.title: Australia forecast to produce a record 2.2 million tonnes of lentils
item.description: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says New Zealand friendship is important to endure volatile world
item.description: Global volatility and economic turbulence have dominated annual talks between the leaders of Australia and New Zealand, Anthony Albanese and Christopher Luxon.

item.title: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato says he was 'a bit too scared' to switch allegiance from Italy
item.description: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato opens up on his decision to switch allegiance from Italy to Australia ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Broncos' season in crisis as Titans young gun conjures golden moment
item.description: Keano Kini scores one of the great game-winning tries to condemn Brisbane to a fifth-straight defeat, with the Titans snatching a 28-23 victory at Lang Park.

item.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas
item.description: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

item.title: Ukraine renews attacks on St Petersburg after Putin rejects Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: A large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targets St Petersburg, with Russia intercepting 376 drones.

item.title: Get a buzz out of spelling? Guy Montgomery is here with your Sunday quiz
item.description: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

item.title: As vaccination rates in Australia decrease, this grieving British mother has a warning
item.description: Measles killed Rebecca Archer's daughter Renae slowly and silently over a decade. She has a message for the growing number of parents hesitant to have their children vaccinated.

item.title: In the great data centre boom, will the benefits flow offshore again?
item.description: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

item.title: How the looksmaxxing social media trend is affecting young men
item.description: While it's a bit of fun for some, the social media trend known as looksmaxxing can have major impacts on young men's physical and mental health.

item.title: From 'rain bombs' to El Niño: Debunking Australia's greatest weather myths and misconceptions
item.description: Meteorological rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

item.title: Stacey Marinkovich: Pressure, pride and the pursuit of Commonwealth Games gold
item.description: The world has never been closer to Australia's Diamonds. Coach Stacey Marinkovich is determined to keep them shining brightest.

item.title: India's viral 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's roads
item.description: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

item.title: Historians vs Donald Trump: The battle for control of presidential records as the US government yearns for the pre-Nixon years
item.description: The Trump administration is pushing for a return to pre-Nixon days when US presidents could do whatever they wanted with presidential records.

item.title: Mirra Andreeva wins French Open after defeating Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 in women's final
item.description: Mirra Andreeva defeats surprise finalist Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest French Open champion in more than three decades.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli claims Monaco Formula 1 Grand Prix pole, Oscar Piastri seventh in qualifying
item.description: Kimi Antonelli makes a mockery of suggestions the Monaco Grand Prix would stall his incredible start to the Formula 1 season, while Oscar Piastri struggles to make an impression.

item.title: Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club knits for calm during ski season
item.description: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

item.title: New technology developed in Canberra could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste each year
item.description: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

item.title: Family remembers Wilson Sprague as passionate stockman and 'good bloke'
item.description: A pilot and talented stockman, Wilson Sprague's family and friends share their story of the "good bloke", who died in a crash in 2025.

item.title: Could the AI boom be a bubble?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wombarra Bowling Club choir singing a Fleetwood Mac song
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Choirs bring community together and offer antidote to loneliness
item.description: As winter isolation sets in, community choirs are providing connection, belonging and emotional support.

item.title: How Tasmania's planned new stadium could trigger deal for the Hobart Hurricanes
item.description: Cricket's smallest state says it could become a major player in privatisation, with a potential Hobart Hurricanes deal tied to Tasmania's planned Macquarie Point roofed stadium.

item.title: Greens senator says Australia cannot defend sea trade lanes, rejecting AUKUS reasoning
item.description: Greens defence spokesman David Shoebridge says it is "ridiculous" to think Australia's military could defend the nation's global trade lanes, and acquiring nuclear-powered submarines under AUKUS only invites Australia to a conflict between the United States and China.

item.title: Private medical imaging provider bills Medicare for non-eligible services
item.description: A private medical imaging provider operating in a public hospital has been billing Medicare for some services that are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

item.title: Australia turns its attention to FIFA World Cup after practice games
item.description: The Socceroos have finished their warm-up games, and now turn their attention to Türkiye in their first World Cup match. So, what have we learned from their friendly games against Mexico and Switzerland?

item.title: Israeli troops kill seven-month-old baby in West Bank, Palestinian officials say
item.description: Israeli troops shot at a car in the occupied West Bank, killing a seven-month-old baby and wounding his parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

item.title: Love story: Accepting my body is my greatest achievement
item.description: I'd take a cure for the pain, but I wouldn't take a cure for my appearance. This is who I am and why should I change myself to make other people more comfortable?

item.title: Running short on soldiers, Russia begins 'aggressive' recruiting drive in educational institutions
item.description: Lessons look a little different at some universities and technical colleges around Russia this year, as the Kremlin tries to entice students to enlist in the military to cover massive casualties in Ukraine.

item.title: Worsening wait times and toxic culture at Ambulance Tasmania revealed, as new pay deal struck
item.description: Average wait times for ambulances in Tasmania have exceeded worst-case scenario projections, despite extra resourcing, with many staff wanting to change careers, new survey results show.

item.title: A 'flamingo revolution' vs a Trump resort. Inside Albania's anti-Kushner protests
item.description: Ivanka Trump has floated "manifestations" of luxury holidays. Meanwhile, Albanians are claiming "irreversible" ecological damage. Why protests are shaking Albania.

item.title: 'Embarrassing' Tigers humiliated by Penrith, Sharks down Dragons
item.description: The Penrith Panthers thrash Wests 68-0 to record the biggest win in club history, as Cronulla notches a 34-12 victory over St George Illawarra at Shark Park.

item.title: Police at Flinders Street Station after a machete brawl
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Blues beat Dons to make it four in a row, Swans pip Saints in a thriller
item.description: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda.

item.title: Former Channel 4 boss says he 'wouldn't have commissioned' Married at First Sight UK
item.description: Married at First Sight UK could be axed as its broadcaster, Channel 4, awaits an external review into the program and serious rape allegations before "deciding what happens next".

item.title: Gold Coast Suns ruck Max Knobel investigated for alleged homophobic slur in VFL match
item.description: The AFL's integrity unit launches an investigation into fringe Gold Coast ruckman Max Knobel for an alleged homophobic slur during a VFL match.

item.title: The legacy of Beethoven's Symphony No. 9 and the composers who came after
item.description: Beethoven's Ninth has topped the ABC Classic 100 countdown for the fifth time. We explore its mystique and shed light on composers following in Beethoven's footsteps.

item.title: Finke Desert Race motorbike rider dies on first day of outback competition from Alice Springs
item.description: A motorbike rider has died in the Central Australian outback after a Finke Desert Race crash, with the two-day event expected to continue as police investigate.

item.title: ACT government to put some infrastructure projects on backburner to help relieve budget pressure
item.description: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

item.title: Race Around The World recap: From Alaska to India, catch the week one films
item.description: From illegal fishing in the Philippines to Michael Jackson impersonators in Las Vegas, catch up on the films from the very first week of Race Around The World.

item.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP
item.description: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

item.title: Almost 950 'outstanding' Australians on 2026 King's Birthday Honours list
item.description: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

item.title: Richard Scolyer dies aged 59, leaving behind legacy of revolutionary brain cancer research
item.description: The 2024 Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

item.title: Hundreds of captives in Nigeria freed from militant group Boko Haram
item.description: Hundreds of men, women and children abducted by militant group Boko Haram have been rescued, according to the Nigerian military.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 8: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?
item.description: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

item.title: Artist Brian Robinson explores everyday magic in Multiverse at Newcastle Art Gallery
item.description: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

item.title: Anthropic sounds the alarm as the AI bubble takes off
item.description: Anthropic wants caution as AI intelligence surges but the stock market can't get enough.

item.title: Federal government accused of AI policy retreat as US tech giants plan Australian investments
item.description: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could lead to harm.

item.title: Parkinson's disease experts warn watchdog as deadline looms on paraquat decision
item.description: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

item.title: Anti-immigration sentiment rises in Japan despite growing demand for foreign workers
item.description: Japan's answer to One Nation skyrockets in popularity after adopting an anti-immigration stance, despite Japan needing foreign workers more than ever.

item.title: Politicians are challenging party lines on migration, gambling and AUKUS
item.description: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

item.title: When Victoria Police unlawfully disclosed her pregnancy, Joanna Adams took it to VCAT and won
item.description: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

item.title: Kim Kardashian watches Lewis Hamilton equal Monaco F1 record, track worries interrupt race
item.description: From a dodgy section of track to a mega celebrity watching her partner equal a record with the great Ayrton Senna, the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix had plenty of talking points.

item.title: Israel retaliates against Iranian missile strikes launched in response to Israeli attack on Beirut
item.description: The Israeli military has fired back at Iran, with state media reporting explosions in three cities after an Iranian strike on northern Israel this morning.

item.title: Denmark's Christian Eriksen collapses during international friendly against Ukraine
item.description: Danish player Christian Eriksen collapses on the pitch in a match against Ukraine but is conscious, the Danish Football Association says in a social media post.

item.title: Alexander Zverev defeats Flavio Cobolli in five sets to win men's French Open final
item.description: In his fourth appearance in the final of a major tournament, Alexander Zverev lifts the French Open trophy following a five-set grind against Flavio Cobolli.

item.title: People aren't moving to Tasmania anymore and that has implications for the economy
item.description: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt. Big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

item.title: Swimming legend Clinton 'Super Fish' Stanley honoured with Medal of the Order of Australia
item.description: A 50th birthday and a Medal of the Order of Australia are all in a month's work for Sunshine Coast swimmer Clinton "Super Fish" Stanley.

item.title: Open letter from Richard Scolyer released following death
item.description: Professor Scolyer wrote he "would be delighted and humbled to be remembered as a proud everyday Aussie who 'gave it a crack', and in doing so, inspired others to pursue their dreams and passions with humility, love and compassion".

item.title: Disability community says the NDIS is 'fighting the wrong battles' over funding plans at review tribunal
item.description: The agency has spent millions on lawyers to fight participants appealing its decisions. Meanwhile, public hearings are due to begin this week into the government's proposal to slash NDIS funding.

item.title: Agritourism operators say highland cow encounters bring 'positive impact'
item.description: Traipsing around a farm in the wake of a highland cow or donkey might seem like a quaint rural pastime, but those in the business of agritourism believe there are other benefits.

item.title: Doug Harrison, 97, refuses to slow down after eight decades as a grazier
item.description: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: Day 100 of this war marked by escalation between Israel and Iran
item.description: This was day 100 of this war, and it's left us with a tinderbox which could be about to explode, writes our correspondent Matthew Doran.

item.title: Australia crowned rugby sevens world champions after conquering New Zealand in Bordeaux final
item.description: Maddison Levi ignores a knee injury to score a superb double as Australia wraps up the women's rugby sevens World Championship in France.

item.title: Does being in a 'safe seat' influence whether your hospital gets an upgrade?
item.description: Are hospitals in safe seats being overlooked for multi-million-dollar refurbishments by the Victorian government?

item.title: 'It's not normal': Indigenous families question 15 deaths in six months in remote Top End community
item.description: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

item.title: Mount Isa cake decorator stops reporting vandalism because 'nothing gets done'
item.description: After three vandalism incidents in three years, cake decorator Ishani Harney says the emotional and financial toll of crime lingers long after the damage is repaired.

item.title: Iran and Israel halt strikes after Trump warns both sides to 'stop shooting'
item.description: The two countries traded large-scale missile attacks in the most direct fighting between the two warring powers since the implementation of April's ceasefire.

item.title: Australia's Kurtis Marschall defeats Armand Duplantis in men's pole vault at Stockholm Diamond League
item.description: Kurtis Marschall pulls off a major upset, defeating world record holder Armand "Mondo" Duplantis at the Stockholm Diamond League meet.

item.title: Rare black-headed gull sighting in Geraldton, WA
item.description: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

item.title: Indigenous siblings pursue careers in medicine, following in the footsteps of their mother
item.description: Ieesha, Abdul and Jamaal Nasser are following in the footsteps of their mother Tatum Maybir, who entered medicine aged 45 after raising her six children.

item.title: Billy Slater to play peacemaker after Maroons stars' fiery club clash
item.description: Queensland coach Billy Slater jokes he might need to table a peace summit between Maroons captain Cameron Munster and fullback Kalyn Ponga as he reveals his team for State of Origin II.

item.title: Critics warn feral pig controls are failing, costing farmers more than $100m
item.description: After an extended dry spell, farmers across inland NSW are reporting significant damage to their paddocks by feral pigs seeking food.

item.title: Charges over 'obscene acts' prompt call for more CCTV in WA's Kimberley
item.description: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

item.title: Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard join Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan in denouncing sexist billboards
item.description: Premier Jacinta Allan has been joined by Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard and other politicians — not including Pauline Hanson — in denouncing the language used to describe her on political signs seen in the Melbourne CBD.

item.title: Three-time Chicago Bulls NBA champion Stacey King dies at 59
item.description: Stacey King, who won NBA titles with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and was a longtime broadcaster for the team, has died.

item.title: Cyclist seriously injured after crash involving police vehicle at St Helens
item.description: Police say a woman riding a motorised bicycle sustained serious injuries in the crash at St Helens.

item.title: Donald Trump storms out of interview after questions on election fraud claims
item.description: US President Donald Trump angrily storms out of an interview with American broadcaster NBC after he was questioned about his unfounded claims of election fraud.

item.title: Perth man wants compensation over damage to apartment during murder investigation
item.description: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants in 2012. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

item.title: WA parliament set to examine mandatory train lighting minimums
item.description: Advocates welcome a WA opposition bill enforcing better train lighting, after decades of campaigning.

item.title: Maroons coach explains Reece Walsh selection
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Magnitude-7.8 earthquake in Philippines causes deaths, damage and tsunami
item.description: At least 35 people have been killed in a magnitude-7.8 earthquake that shook the Mindanao region in the southern Philippines, with a tsunami possible on some regional coasts.

item.title: Nelly Korda wins US Women's Open title by one stroke ahead of Charley Hull and Gaby Lopez
item.description: Nelly Korda claims the US Women's Open for her second consecutive major victory, holding off Charley Hull and Gaby Lopez by one shot in Los Angeles.

item.title: Cancer expert Richard Scolyer remembered as loving family man making a difference to lives of patients
item.description: The family of former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer have paid tribute to his legacy, saying he "wanted cancer patients to know they weren't alone".

item.title: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas has 'open mind' about working with One Nation
item.description: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

item.title: UK, France and Germany back Zelenskyy's call for meeting with Putin
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has received the backing of the leaders of the UK, France and Germany in calling for direct ceasefire talks with Russia.

item.title: Tasmanian MPs push premier for answers about Madeleine Ogilvie
item.description: Seven crossbench MPs have written to Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff to demand answers about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's resignation from the ministry, giving him until Friday morning to provide "direct and exact answers".

item.title: Magistrate extends order to suppress name of high-profile Queensland man at centre of extortion case
item.description: A magistrate has extended an order preventing the publication of details of the alleged extortion case, including the identities of the people involved.

item.title: Titan Ridge volcano in Papua New Guinea inundates Manus coastlines with pumice rocks
item.description: Residents are concerned about access to food and fresh water as pumice makes travel difficult for Papua New Guinea's seafaring communities.

item.title: Iran launches missiles towards northern Israel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mihocek experienced 'lack of feeling' before hospital visit says coach
item.description: Melbourne claims an eight-point win over Collingwood at the MCG as Brody Mihocek is taken to hospital after a serious injury.

item.title: SA government says illicit tobacco prices increasing in response to store crackdown
item.description: South Australia's Consumer and Business Affairs Minister says a price increase for illegal tobacco noticed by inspectors within stores shows the trade is being disrupted.

item.title: Sunshine Coast father in custody for allegedly critically harming nine-week-old son
item.description: A 30-year-old Queensland man has faced court today, charged with permanently and critically injuring his nine-week-old son.

item.title: NSW town of Wentworth sets vintage tractor record 70 years on from flood
item.description: A New South Wales town hosts a celebration of the "little grey" tractors that helped save it from flooding 70 years ago.

item.title: Nestory Irankunda the face of the Socceroos at FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Nestory Irankunda is set for a breakout moment at the FIFA World Cup as the face of a changing Socceroos team featuring six players of African descent.

item.title: Five tasty ways to use cheap lentils in your meal plan
item.description: From soups to bolognese and oven bakes, lentils are a versatile and affordable meal option.

item.title: Sam Pang on his new comedy series Ground Up
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jai Wright's family fight 'never about a sentence' in rare moment of accountability
item.description: Inside a quiet district courtroom in the heart of Sydney’s bustling CBD, a historic moment has unfolded involving a NSW Police sergeant.

item.title: As One Nation nips at his heels the PM has no choice but to play the long game
item.description: The government is playing a long game in responding to the surge of support for One Nation. It has little other choice.

item.title: 'Brazen' Illawarra hammer attack leaves man with fractured skull
item.description: Police on the NSW south coast are investigating an alleged hammer attack that has left a man with a fractured skull and bleeding on the brain.

item.title: Missiles fly over rally in Kermanshah
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Perth paediatrician Chia Liang Saw faces court on alleged child sex charges
item.description: A paediatrician charged with child sex offences may have up to nine victims, with alleged offending captured on "hidden cameras" in consulting rooms, a Perth court hears.

item.title: Blues centre Stephen Crichton ruled out of State of Origin II
item.description: New South Wales have been dealt an injury blow ahead of State of Origin with Stephen Crichton ruled out of the squad due to a shoulder injury.

item.title: Clare mayor express shock at man's death during arrest, after 'ineffective' taser activation
item.description: A local mayor has expressed shock at the death of a man during an arrest at Clare, in South Australia's Mid North on Sunday, as the state's police minister says he is confident the incident will be thoroughly investigated.

item.title: First Big Freeze held at the MCG since the death of AFL legend and MND campaigner Neale Daniher
item.description: The MCG has hosted the first Big Freeze MND fundraiser since the death of AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner, Neale Daniher.

item.title: Javier Bardem, Amy Adams, and Patrick Wilson on the remake of Cape Fear
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Giancarlo Esposito on his most villainous roles
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Albany community grapples with fatal shark attack that claimed 35yo resident
item.description: The Albany community is still coming to terms with the loss of resident Daniel Turpin after he did not survive a shark attack at the weekend.

item.title: Barunga Festival 2026: Groote Eylandt man nails spear-throwing contest at range of 35 metres
item.description: Sammy Wurrawilya has taken out the title of spear-throwing champion at this year's Barunga Festival with an impressive effort of more than 30 metres.

item.title: Damage to Israeli settlement of Itamar in West Bank
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian dollar dives to two-month low as interest rate fears grip markets
item.description: The Australian dollar falls to its lowest level in over two months, just over 70 cents to the US dollar, amid a renewed surge in the greenback as global interest rates, including US interest rates, rise.

item.title: Modern waste-to-energy plants claim to be safe but these Melbourne residents aren't convinced
item.description: Australian states are pushing ahead with waste-to-energy incineration despite significant opposition. It means one capital city will soon be surrounded by a "ring of fire".

item.title: Finke Desert Race legends Travis Robinson and David Walsh win again, alongside reigning champs Hannah Bentley and Madi Healey
item.description: Returning legends of the Finke Desert Race, David Walsh and Travis Robinson, have been crowned Kings of the Desert at the gruelling two-day off-road race, with Walsh winning on bikes for a record-equalling sixth time and Robinson claiming victory on cars.

item.title: Man killed while competing in the Finke Desert Race identfied as Matthew Bielefeld
item.description: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

item.title: Bendigo police charge a further three teens following alleged stabbing of homeless man
item.description: Police charge a further three teens following the alleged stabbing of a homeless man in the regional Victorian city of Bendigo last week.

item.title: Police reveal scam script found in Cambodian centre targeting Australians
item.description: Australian and Thai police release a script discovered in a Cambodian scam compound, revealing the high-pressure methods used to coerce Australian victims.

item.title: Israeli Deputy Foreign Minister speaks on the escalating unrest in the Middle East
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: The amazing legacy of Professor Richard Scolyer
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Armenia elects pro-West Civil Contract party despite pressure from Russia
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party won 49.8% of votes, enough to secure a parliamentary majority under Armenia's electoral system.

item.title: Chinese leader Xi Jinping pledges deeper cooperation with North Korea's Kim Jong Un during rare visit
item.description: China and North Korea should strengthen exchanges "in diplomacy, law enforcement, and the military", Chinese leader Xi Jinping has suggested during a rare two-day visit to Pyongyang.

item.title: Iran and Israel cease fighting but warn future attacks will trigger retaliation
item.description: Israel and Iran have agreed to hold off attacks but Iran says it won't "retreat from any threat' with Israel too warning it would "respond with full force" if attacks resumed.

item.title: Author Josh Silver channels Yellowface in satirical novel examining privilege and queer identity
item.description: Described as one of the books of the year, Fruit Fly asks who has the right to tell which stories in an age of cancellation for cultural appropriation.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: Kings and Queens edition
item.description: The latest ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to rank some of the most famous (and infamous) royals in history, and sort some of the current crop.

item.title: Have you found mould in your rental? Here's what to do next
item.description: Who is responsible for a mould issue depends on a few factors, but experts say it is worth acting early on.

item.title: Australians less satisfied with life than during pandemic as financial pressures mount
item.description: Australians felt a greater sense of satisfaction during the COVID-19 pandemic than they do today, according to government data, as households grapple with the impact of inflation, rising interest rates and a reduction in real wages.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: From four-time veterans to late bolter Volpato, here's your Socceroos World Cup guide
item.description: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

item.title: Property and the budget ease pressure on the RBA as two banks cut fixed rates
item.description: With two Australian banks cutting fixed home loan rates, there is a ray of hope that the cycle of interest rate hikes has peaked and borrowing costs may be lower down the track — another sign the pace of economic change has moved into overdrive.

item.title: The most contentious parts of the NDIS bill being scrutinised today
item.description: Politicians are presiding over three days of public hearings into a bill paving the way for the biggest ever cuts to the NDIS.

item.title: US and Iran negotiations in 'deadlock', but ships are finding ways to cross the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Amid new strikes, the future of the Strait of Hormuz has again been plunged into uncertainty, but ships are finding a way through.

item.title: The Taiwan crisis no one saw coming, and it has nothing to do with war
item.description: Taipei sits at the physical centre of the global AI economy but remains shut out of the diplomatic rooms where its sovereign rights are negotiated, often by others.

item.title: Couple launches youth housing organisation to combat Sunshine Coast rental crisis
item.description: Hopeful renters say negative stereotypes about young people are making it almost impossible to secure a rental on the Sunshine Coast.

item.title: Fire insiders alarmed over data centre expansion in Melbourne's inner west
item.description: Serious concerns about an influx of large-scale data centres in Melbourne's west are being raised by Fire Rescue Victoria insiders.

item.title: Pope Leo meets abuse victims in Spain while some protest
item.description: The pontiff, who is on a week-long visit to Spain, has listened to proposals to make safeguarding more effective.

item.title: Record autumn rain sparks recovery across South Australia, but not all growers benefit
item.description: Twelve months after drought left paddocks bare, record rainfall in parts of South Australia has left some farmers smiling at significant improvements.
